title: accessing 2D array defined through generate command
body:
I have a module that generates a memory with a nested generate command. 
The final array is a 2D array with 32x4 elements each one on 8 bits.
I see that this array is not automatically dumped using $dumpvars.
I the assigned the elements to a wire and dumped them. 
The results are tricky.  The varius elements are visualized correctly with the execption of the element with [0,0] index.

Below the example of the code I put in the TB.

// definition of the 2D array that accesses to the internal hierarchy
wire [31:0] MEM [31:0];
genvar ii;
generate
for (ii=0;ii<32;ii=ii+1)
	assign MEM[ii] = {UUT.Mycirc.BRAM_GEN[0].BYTE_BRAM_GEN[3].byte_ram[ii],
				UUT.Mycirc.BRAM_GEN[0].BYTE_BRAM_GEN[2].byte_ram[ii],
				UUT.Mycirc.BRAM_GEN[0].BYTE_BRAM_GEN[1].byte_ram[ii],
				UUT.Mycirc.BRAM_GEN[0].BYTE_BRAM_GEN[0].byte_ram[ii]};
endgenerate

// code for the initial block
initial
begin
$dumpfile("waveform.vcd");
$dumpvars;
for (idx = 0; idx < 32; idx = idx + 1) $dumpvars(0, MEM[idx]);

Everything works fine with the MEM elements from 1 to 31. They are visualized and when I chenge the values they behave accordingly.
But when I work with the element MEM[0] only the higher 16bits are changed and the other ones remain xxxxxx.
It looks like there is problem in conncting the MEM0] with the elements:
UUT.Mycirc.BRAM_GEN[0].BYTE_BRAM_GEN[1].byte_ram[0],
UUT.Mycirc.BRAM_GEN[0].BYTE_BRAM_GEN[0].byte_ram[0]

* It is not a visualization probelm. I checke the vcd file visualized by GTKwave and it stores the wrong result.
* the same code is simulate in Mdelsim and provides correct results also for the MEM0] element.

Any hint?

Thx
